🩺 Telemetry Health Bundle

Three changes: a dead-writer liveness guard (SessionStart hook), a routing_edge SQLite view (migration 003), and doctor/analytics doc wiring. Motivated by a real incident: skill-usage.jsonl wrote 1 entry between 2026-03-07 and 2026-07-13 while agent-usage.jsonl logged 245–1,132 events/day. Nothing warned.

Simulator: when does the guard fire?

Exact logic of findDeadWriters(): warn only when a writer is stale (≥48h) while a sibling is fresh (≤24h). Drag the ages and watch the verdict.

routing_edge view · why the window is load-bearing

The auto routers hand off by invoking the target skill, so a handoff shows up as the next skill_invocation row in the session. The first cut of this view left that join unbounded — and adversarial review caught that it was measuring the wrong thing entirely. Measured against the real DB:

gap between router call and next skillpairs
≤ 30s (plausible same-turn handoff)2
≤ 2m — the shipped bound3
≤ 15m21
unbounded (the original view)53

Median unbounded gap: 43 minutes. Max: 27 hours. Those are not handoffs — they are just the next thing the user ran in a long multi-goal session. Bounding at 120s and excluding router→same-router self-pairs cuts 53 apparent edges to 3 real ones. The view now ships as an explicit lower bound: a router that answers inline, or dispatches into a forked subagent whose skill calls land under another session id, produces no row at all. Decision-level routing (goal → chosen intent) is in-prompt and written nowhere; capturing it needs a dedicated telemetry stream, which this view does not pretend to be.

src/skills/doctor/SKILL.md · analytics/references/jq-queries.mddoc wiring
